hotel.name: Hotel de la Poste – French Quarter, A Renaissance Hotel
perks: Daily city tours for 2 (French Quarter, Garden District, Voodoo/Haunted History, Beignet package)
hotel.parking: Valet only — $55/day standard, $65/day oversized (SUV+). 78" height limit. No self-parking.
hotel.pool: Heated outdoor pool, 9 AM – 10 PM. Brick courtyard with fountain & fire feature.
hotel.dining_onsite: 3rd Block Depot Kitchen+Bar — Southern Creole street food, breakfast/lunch/dinner
hotel.pet_policy: Up to 2 pets (40 lbs max each), $150 non-refundable fee per stay
room_types: Expanded Room (courtyard or French Quarter views)
Suite with Private Balcony (French Quarter views)
hotel.booking_tip: Wednesday/Tuesday nights tend to be cheapest. Thursday is typically the most expensive. July is peak summer — book early.
hotel.notes: Only 97 rooms — intimate boutique feel. Jewel-toned bayou-inspired decor. Steps from Jackson Square & Bourbon St. The included Voodoo/Haunted History tour is a perfect fit for Freedom's aesthetic. Walkable location negates the need for a car.

activities.name: Ragin' Cajun Airboat Tours
activities.price: Small Airboat: $79 self-drive / $99 w/ hotel pickup | Large Airboat: $60 / $79 | Combo (Airboat + Plantation): $165–$175 w/ shuttle
activities.includes: Hotel shuttle to/from French Quarter (25 min ride)
activities.restrictions: No children under 5, pregnant women, or individuals with serious medical conditions
activities.wildlife: American Alligators, Bald Eagles, Raccoons, Wild Boar, Turtles, Snakes, Spanish Moss draped Live Oaks
activities.cancellation: Free cancellation up to 24 hours in advance
activities.notes: 100% private property tour deep in Louisiana swamps. July is prime gator season — sightings very likely. Weather-dependent with refund/reschedule policy.

activities.name: Museum of Death
activities.closed: Thanksgiving, Christmas Day, Mardi Gras Day
activities.payment: Cashless only (credit/debit/tap). No advance/online tickets – in person only.
exhibits: Serial Killer Room — letters & memorabilia (Bundy, Gacy, etc.)
Kevorkian Machine — original device with '60 Minutes' interview
Funeral & Embalming — Victorian mourning rings, face casts (Napoleon, Lincoln)
activities.fun_fact: Visitors who faint receive a free t-shirt: 'I passed out at the Museum of Death...and lived to talk about it'
activities.notes: U-shaped gallery path through intimately packed rooms. Plan 45–60 min. Go early on a weekday (Mon–Wed) to avoid lines.

shopping.name: Dark Matter Oddities & Artisan Collective
shopping.description: Oddities shop and collective of visual and taxidermy artists. Supports emerging and established local/regional artists from the Gulf Coast.
shopping.notes: Steps from Jackson Square. Small but curated – a must for oddity lovers.

shopping.name: Marie Laveau's House of Voodoo
shopping.price: Free to browse | $50 for 20-min tarot reading
shopping.psychic_readings: Daily noon–10 PM (later on Fri/Sat). Sign up early on busy days.
shopping.description: Voodoo museum and shop since 1978. Voodoo altar, spiritual items, books, and artifacts from around the world.
shopping.notes: Recently moved from 739 to 628 Bourbon St. Arrive early for tarot readings.

shopping.name: Boutique du Vampyre
shopping.address: 709½ St. Ann St, New Orleans, LA 70116
shopping.description: The only brick-and-mortar vampire shop in America. Custom-made fangs by fangsmith Maven Lore, coffin-shaped backpacks, Famous Fortune Candles (handmade with hidden pewter charms), vampire dolls, capes, Vampire Wine & Vodka, VampFire Hot Sauce, skull sugar cubes, and one-of-a-kind curiosities. Run by Marita Jaeger, the 'Queen of New Orleans Vampires,' since 2003.
shopping.extras: Photo op in a standing coffin. Adjacent Vampire Café with mythology-inspired cocktails.
shopping.notes: This is also the gateway to Potions Lounge speakeasy — staff here whisper the password. Ranked #1 in its category on TripAdvisor. Staff (especially Steven) receive exceptional praise.

dining.name: Vampire Apothecary
dining.address: 725 St Peter St, New Orleans, LA 70116
menu_highlights: Crab Toast (3pc) – lump crab, red peppers, Apothecary sauce, bowfin caviar on sourdough
Beef Short Rib Po'Boy – slow-cooked short ribs, Provolone, garlic aioli on French bread
Tofu Bowl – marinated tofu, Brussels sprouts, coconut curry aioli (GF, VEG)
Grilled Cheese – Provolone on brioche, tomato, truffle oil
Brownie with hazelnut praline filling & chocolate mirror glaze
Pumpkin spice cheesecake with speculoos crust & salted caramel
drink_highlights: Absinthe – first distilled in NOLA since 1912 ban, infused with hibiscus (lush red)
Aphrodisiac Bloody Mary – Vampire Vodka with fresh oyster
dining.extras: Custom fang fittings and tarot readings on premises. Courtyard garden herbs used in dishes.
dining.notes: Connected to Boutique du Vampyre. Herbs and flowers from their own courtyard garden. Many dishes have medicinal properties.

dining.name: The Court of Two Sisters – Jazz Brunch
includes: 1 complimentary glass of wine, champagne, or mimosa
menu_highlights: Carving Station: Breakfast Ham (9–11 AM), Ribeye (11 AM–3 PM)
Bananas Foster, bread pudding, pecan pie
dining.dress_code: Nice casual. No sleeveless shirts or cut-off shorts.
dining.reservations: Recommended, especially for courtyard seating. Book online.
dining.notes: Historic French Quarter courtyard. Live jazz trio plays throughout brunch. A NOLA institution.

nightlife.name: Potions Lounge – The Speakeasy
nightlife.cover: $10 (may increase to $20 later in the evening)
nightlife.how_to_enter: Visit Boutique du Vampyre at 709½ St. Ann St first. Browse the shop, and a vampire staff member will whisper the password and secret location.
nightlife.specialties: Absinthe, craft cocktails, tarot readings, magic shows
nightlife.vibe: 1930s Paris-style bar with balcony overlooking Bourbon Street. Vampire owned and operated.
nightlife.membership: Available – includes unlimited free entry, guest privileges, private party access (4–8 PM), VIP event access.
nightlife.notes: The password ritual through Boutique du Vampyre is part of the experience. Go early for a chill vibe, later for energy.

nightlife.name: The Carousel Bar & Lounge
nightlife.address: 214 Royal St, New Orleans, LA 70130 (inside Hotel Monteleone)
nightlife.reservations: Not accepted. First-come, first-served. Only 25 carousel seats.
nightlife.dress_code: No formal dress code. Suggested: casual date night.
nightlife.description: The only revolving bar in New Orleans. The carousel makes one full rotation every 15 minutes while you sip world-class cocktails.
nightlife.tip: Go in the afternoon to snag a carousel seat. Evenings and weekends get packed.
nightlife.hotel_guest_perk: Exclusive early entry at 10 AM (1 hour before public opening).
nightlife.notes: Open to all – no hotel stay required. A NOLA landmark and literary haunt (Hemingway, Faulkner, Tennessee Williams were regulars).
